channel.title: Digg AI Feed from X
channel.description: Top 10 AI stories from Digg's X-ranked tech feed with click-through links to original source content.

item.title: OpenAI launches GPT-Live voice models inside ChatGPT to enable more natural and interruption-resistant conversations
item.description: The immediate rollout follows a teaser featuring a 2026 development credit.
item.content:encoded:
The immediate rollout follows a teaser featuring a 2026 development credit.

Original source: https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-live/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/ffcsue5m

  • Views: 1,671,057
  • Likes: 18,972
  • Bookmarks: 2,127
  • Quotes: 156
  • Replies: 1,557
  • Posts: 54

Top authors

  • Sam Altman
  • Greg Brockman
  • Boaz Barak
  • Peter Steinberger 🦞
  • Robert Scoble
As someone who uses voice (wispr) extensively, I have to say that gpt voice up til now has been terrible. I can barely get a word in. I am hyped they are addressing it.

item.title: Prime Intellect raises $130 million Series A from Radical Ventures, NVIDIA, Intel, and Dell to build open AI infrastructure
item.description: The funds will back its enterprise AI training and deployment stack
item.content:encoded:
The funds will back its enterprise AI training and deployment stack

Original source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/08/prime-intellect-raises-130m-series-a-to-help-enterprises-build-their-own-ai-agents/?utm_source=dlvr.it&utm_medium=twitter

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/8tasmgcq

  • Views: 436,978
  • Likes: 4,075
  • Bookmarks: 518
  • Quotes: 17
  • Replies: 328
  • Posts: 75

Top authors

  • Miles Brundage
  • Dwarkesh Patel
  • Delip Rao e/σ
  • swyx
  • Yu Su
We're excited to be joined by angels who are building the frontier themselves, many of whom we work closely with: @johnschulman2 (Thinking Machines), @dwarkesh_sp, @AravSrinivas (Perplexity), @karimatiyeh (Ramp), @levie (Box), @_milankovac_ (Tesla), @winstonweinberg (Harvey), @amspector100 (Flapping Airplanes), @jeffwang (Cognition), @_arohan_ (Core Automation), @marksaroufim (Core Automation), @mikeknoop (Zapier, Ndea), @eastdakota (Cloudflare), @BrendanFoody (Mercor), @devanshpandey (Standard Intelligence), @hwchase17 (Langchain), @nicoup (Fleet) and many more

item.title: OpenAI will launch GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna models publicly this Thursday
item.description: Early testers report major improvements in math and coding.
item.content:encoded:
Early testers report major improvements in math and coding.

Original source: https://cryptobriefing.com/openai-to-launch-gpt-56-sol-terra-luna-models-globally-this-thursday/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/65f5lo27

  • Views: 213,231
  • Likes: 3,350
  • Bookmarks: 247
  • Quotes: 11
  • Replies: 126
  • Posts: 16

Top authors

  • Sebastien Bubeck
  • Boris Power
  • kache
  • Beff (e/acc)
  • Bojan Tunguz
@OpenAI

item.title: Cognition releases SWE-1.7, scoring 42.3 on FrontierCode benchmark using reinforcement learning on a Kimi base
item.description: The model runs at 1,000 tokens per second on Cerebras.
item.content:encoded:
The model runs at 1,000 tokens per second on Cerebras.

Original source: https://devin.ai/cli

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/29irz4lv

  • Views: 136,472
  • Likes: 1,520
  • Bookmarks: 243
  • Quotes: 11
  • Replies: 80
  • Posts: 22

Top authors

  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • Scott Wu
  • Josh Wolfe
  • Danielle Fong 🔆
  • Lisan al Gaib
SWE-1.7 was built on broad improvements in RL pipeline on top of a Kimi K2.7 base model. Our proprietary benchmark FrontierCode evaluates whether a model makes code you’d actually want to merge. SWE-1.7 advances the cost-performance Pareto curve on FrontierCode with a score of 42.3% and cost per task of $1.97 on our Main set.

item.title: OpenAI says SWE-Bench Pro has hit a 70% noise ceiling and no longer reliably measures frontier model capabilities
item.description: The benchmark's saturation is driving calls for proprietary evaluations.
item.content:encoded:
The benchmark's saturation is driving calls for proprietary evaluations.

Original source: https://openai.com/index/separating-signal-from-noise-coding-evaluations/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/h8fave4h

  • Views: 105,061
  • Likes: 1,049
  • Bookmarks: 79
  • Quotes: 2
  • Replies: 66
  • Posts: 15

Top authors

  • Wenhu Chen
  • kache
  • Matei Zaharia
  • Robert Scoble
  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
To audit SWE-Bench Pro, we used model-based investigator agents alongside independent reviews from five independent experienced software engineers. That helped us examine tasks at scale while keeping expert judgment at the center.

item.title: Bun creator Jarred Sumner announces the JavaScript runtime is being completely rewritten from Zig to Rust
item.description: The migration at Anthropic aims to improve long-term project maintenance.
item.content:encoded:
The migration at Anthropic aims to improve long-term project maintenance.

Original source: https://bun.com/blog/bun-in-rust

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/upyr56rx

  • Views: 696,610
  • Likes: 4,633
  • Bookmarks: 1,825
  • Quotes: 207
  • Replies: 275
  • Posts: 9

Top authors

  • Sasha Rush
  • Boris Cherny
  • Thariq
  • Theo - t3.gg
  • Jarred Sumner
This is pretty epic

item.title: Paradigm co-founder Matt Huang launches $1.2 billion fund targeting crypto, AI, and robotics
item.description: The fourth fund marks an expansion into broader deep tech.
item.content:encoded:
The fourth fund marks an expansion into broader deep tech.

Original source: https://x.com/paradigm/status/2074871132805861802/video/1

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/o7sce980

  • Views: 344,655
  • Likes: 2,135
  • Bookmarks: 406
  • Quotes: 42
  • Replies: 287
  • Posts: 13

Top authors

  • Brian Armstrong
  • signüll
  • Alexis Ohanian 🗽
  • Matt Huang
  • Turner Novak 🍌🧢
Matt’s the best.

item.title: Mitchell Hashimoto says Sol 5.6 is faster than Fable and has become his default AI coding agent
item.description: The HashiCorp co-founder still reserves Fable for specialized debugging tasks.
item.content:encoded:
The HashiCorp co-founder still reserves Fable for specialized debugging tasks.

Original source: https://www.reuters.com/technology/openai-gets-us-approval-broad-gpt-56-rollout-axios-reports-2026-07-08/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/yjyltmlm

  • Views: 427,163
  • Likes: 5,502
  • Bookmarks: 1,198
  • Quotes: 82
  • Replies: 188
  • Posts: 7

Top authors

  • Boaz Barak
  • Eric
  • elvis
  • Mitchell Hashimoto
  • Pietro Schirano
mitchell hashmioto, famously also on OpenAI's payroll apparently.

item.title: Reports suggest OpenAI plans to release GPT-6 by late summer to compete with Anthropic's Fable 5.1
item.description: The model is reportedly built on a massive new pretraining run
item.content:encoded:
The model is reportedly built on a massive new pretraining run

Original source: https://help.openai.com/en/articles/9624314-model-release-notes

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/3y2g53c3

  • Views: 283,505
  • Likes: 3,363
  • Bookmarks: 419
  • Quotes: 23
  • Replies: 139
  • Posts: 11

Top authors

  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • Andrew Curran
  • Lisan al Gaib
  • Chubby♨️
  • Ramez Naam
OpenAI is back baby "GPT-6 will be based on a new, significantly larger pretrain than GPT-5.5/GPT-5.6"

item.title: Every co-founder Dan Shipper says GPT-5.6 excels at routine tasks while Fable acts as a warp drive for massive projects
item.description: The initial model comparison will be followed by a detailed evaluation.
item.content:encoded:
The initial model comparison will be followed by a detailed evaluation.

Original source: https://finance.biggo.com/podcast/69b31788e7a62de3

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/lyentuzn

  • Views: 582,153
  • Likes: 5,085
  • Bookmarks: 983
  • Quotes: 74
  • Replies: 289
  • Posts: 17

Top authors

  • Siqi Chen
  • Dan Shipper 📧
  • Alex Volkov @ AI Engineer
  • Charlie Marsh
@danshipper @every Yes but is 5.6 prose better? It is easy to talk to? Does it write well?
